scenario: Pillar Bank runs Camunda 8.8 SaaS for 12000 loans per day. A newly onboarded operator opens the Processes view in Operate, expands the operations panel for the first time on a clean tenant where no cancel, retry, migration, or modification action has ever been started, and wonders what the empty operations panel should display.
question: What does the operations panel show when no cancel, retry, migration, or modification action has yet been started?

options.text: The panel displays the text No operations have been created yet. and stays empty until an operator initiates an operation
The panel displays a default seed list of system-generated operations created automatically by Zeebe at deploy time
The panel displays the last 5 operations from the prior Operate version that were migrated forward from a Camunda 7 setup
The panel displays the cluster job worker activity feed so the operator can pick a stuck job and act on it from there

a.text: Correct. Per docs, if no operations exist the operations panel shows the text No operations have been created yet. and remains empty until an operator initiates a cancel, retry, migration, or modification action in the cluster.

b.text: Incorrect. Zeebe does not seed system-generated operations at deploy time; the operations panel only lists operator-initiated cancel, retry, migration, or modification actions.

c.text: Incorrect. Operate does not import operations from a Camunda 7 engine; the panel reflects only operations created by operators in the current Camunda 8 cluster.

d.text: Incorrect. The operations panel lists operator-initiated cancel, retry, migration, and modification actions on process instances; it is not a job worker activity feed.

explanation: On a tenant where nothing has yet been started, the Operate docs say the operations panel shows the text No operations have been created yet., and entries appear only after an operator triggers a cancel, retry, migration, or modification action.
